mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level versus 60-day forward returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and increases in the unemployment rate signal a joint contraction in consumer spending and household income that amplifies negative earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE does not add incremental explanatory power (delta R-squared < 0.01) over main effects for 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an accelerating consumer price index forces equity markets to reprice higher terminal interest rate expectations, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified tightening cycles with CPIAUCSL > 3.5%, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts of more than five percent signals weakening residential construction activity and negative household wealth effects that reduce aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines > 5% MoM are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding corporate output and operating leverage that translates into higher earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth exhibits a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of two consecutive monthly increases in the unemployment rate alongside a stagnant or declining federal funds rate reveals a policy-constrained negative labor market shock that suppresses consumption and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and flat or lower FEDFUNDS does not produce significantly negative 5-day average returns (t-test p > 0.10 relative to zero).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth during a period of restrictive monetary policy causes the Federal Reserve to maintain elevated interest rates for longer than priced, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong RSAFS and elevated FEDFUNDS is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
